title: SSA Reasoner – Cooking Substitutions Delta
description: Delta-style output schema for cooking ingredient substitutions. Used by the Reasoner to propose, refine, or deprecate substitution rules the Session engine can apply when composing cooking sessions.

schemaVersion.description: Semantic version for this schema’s payload shape.

domain.description: Domain for which these substitutions apply. Fixed to 'cooking' for this schema.

generatedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this substitutions delta was generated.

requestId.description: Optional ID that ties this delta back to a Reasoner request.

householdId.description: Optional household or profile identifier this delta is scoped to.

locale.description: BCP-47 locale in which ingredient names and notes are expressed, e.g. 'en-US'.

substitutions.description: List of individual substitution rules to add or modify.

id.description: Stable identifier for this substitution rule. May reference an existing rule for updates.

status.description: Whether this substitution is newly proposed, already accepted, or deprecated.

operation.description: How this delta should be applied to the substitutions knowledge base.

id.description: Optional canonical id for the base ingredient (if available from inventory/recipe DB).

name.description: Human-facing name of the base ingredient, e.g. 'buttermilk'.

tags.description: Free-form tags for matching and clustering, e.g. ['dairy', 'acidic', 'liquid'].

id.description: Optional canonical id for the substitute ingredient.

name.description: Human-facing name of the substitute ingredient, e.g. 'whole milk + vinegar'.

tags.description: Free-form tags for matching and clustering, e.g. ['dairy', 'acidic', 'liquid'].

ratio.description: Multiplicative ratio for quantity conversion: substituteQuantity = baseQuantity * ratio. Use 1.0 for 1:1 substitutions.

unitHint.description: Optional unit hint if the ratio is defined for specific units (e.g. 'cup', 'tablespoon', 'gram').

confidence.description: 0–1 numeric confidence score representing how safe/standard this substitution is.

origin.description: Where this substitution knowledge came from.

sourceType.description: Type of source that produced this substitution.

ref.description: Optional reference id (e.g. recipe id, import id, user id, rule id).

contexts.description: Contextual constraints where this substitution is recommended or allowed.

dietary.description: Dietary patterns for which the substitution is appropriate.

allergyRisk.description: Allergy risk information for this substitution.

introducesNewAllergen.description: Whether this substitute introduces an allergen that the original ingredient did not have.

notes.description: Free-form explanation of allergy risks, if any.

flavorProfile.description: Flavor profile tags relevant to the substitution, e.g. ['smoky', 'sweet', 'savory'].

textureImpact.description: Summary of how the substitution will impact texture.

equipmentConstraints.description: Equipment assumptions for this substitution to work as intended, e.g. 'stand-mixer', 'oven', 'pressure-cooker'.

methodConstraints.description: Cooking methods where this substitution is valid, e.g. ['bake', 'saute', 'braise'].

recommendedAdjustments.description: Optional recommendations for adjusting the recipe/session steps when this substitution is applied.

timeSecDelta.description: Suggested change to cook time in seconds for affected steps (can be negative).

tempFDiff.description: Suggested change in temperature (°F) when using this substitution (can be negative).

notes.description: Human-readable adjustment notes to surface in the SessionRunner (e.g. 'Check earlier for doneness; sugar substitutes can brown faster.').

guardImplications.description: Hints about how this substitution interacts with Session guards (inventory, Sabbath, quiet hours, etc.).

affectsInventory.description: True if using this substitution can resolve an inventory blocker (e.g. missing original ingredient).

canLowerNoise.description: True if this substitution tends to reduce noisy equipment usage (helpful for quiet hours).

torahRelevant.description: True if this substitution helps align with Torah food-cleanliness or feast-day constraints.

notes.description: User-facing description of the substitution, including caveats and best-use cases.

createdAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this substitution rule was first created.

updatedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this substitution rule was last updated.
